message:
fix(runtime): normalize protocol error codes, reject non-finite CLI floats, fix disabled hugepage evaluation

Three independent correctness fixes across the runtime:

CLI argument parsing (cli.rs):
- is_negative_numeric_token() now uses is_ok_and(f64::is_finite) to
  reject NaN and Infinity tokens that previously parsed as valid floats,
  preventing them from being misinterpreted as negative numeric arguments

Extension dispatcher (extension_dispatcher.rs):
- protocol_error_code() and protocol_error_fallback_reason() now
  normalize input with trim().to_ascii_lowercase() before matching,
  making protocol error classification case- and whitespace-insensitive
  (e.g., " TIMEOUT " and "Timeout" both map correctly)
- Add tests: protocol_error_code_normalizes_case_and_whitespace,
  protocol_error_fallback_reason_normalizes_code_before_taxonomy_mapping,
  and comprehensive protocol_params_from_request tests covering tool,
  exec, http, session, ui, events, and log hostcall kinds

Scheduler hugepage logic (scheduler.rs):
- Fix set_hugepage_status() to call HugepageStatus::evaluate() when
  hugepages are disabled, instead of blindly accepting the external
  status which could falsely report active=true
- Disabled config now takes precedence over alignment checks, producing
  HugepageFallbackReason::Disabled consistently
- Add tests: disabled_hugepage_config_rejects_external_active_status,
  disabled_uses_disabled_reason_even_if_misaligned,
  zero_page_size_fails_closed, zero_footprint_fails_closed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
